unit.name: Sequence modeling and RNN motivation
unit.description: Introduces variable-length sequence modeling and the standard input-output task regimes.
unit.summary: The lecture shifts from fixed-size inputs/outputs to sequence modeling, where inputs and/or outputs may have variable length [ts=289s]. RNNs and variants are introduced as the main simple neural sequence models used before transformers, with key ideas still appearing in state-space models such as Mamba [ts=303s] [ts=313s]. The instructor distinguishes one-to-many image captioning, many-to-one video classification, and many-to-many sequence mappings [ts=351s] [ts=377s] [ts=401s]. For explaining RNN mechanics, the lecture focuses on the aligned many-to-many case where each input step has a corresponding output, such as frame-level video classification [ts=422s].

key_points.text: Sequence modeling handles variable-length inputs or outputs rather than only fixed-size vectors.
RNN ideas remain relevant in modern state-space models such as Mamba.
One-to-many maps an image to a variable-length caption; many-to-one maps video frames to one class.
The lecture initially focuses on aligned many-to-many RNNs.

kp_catalog.name: Sequence modeling regimes
kp_catalog.description: One-to-many, many-to-one, and many-to-many formulations for variable-length input/output tasks.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because sequence modeling regimes formalizes how one-to-many, many-to-one, and many-to-many formulations for variable-length input/output tasks, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Modern RNN-like state-space models
kp_catalog.description: RWKV, Mamba, and related models that revive RNN-like linear-time sequence modeling.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because modern RNN-like state-space models synthesizes rWKV, Mamba, and related models that revive RNN-like linear-time sequence modeling into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.
